prop_logic.label: Propositional Logic
prop_logic.desc: Truth tables, connectives, tautologies, CNF/DNF, natural deduction. The floor of every formal discipline.

books.n: 12 pages that founded modern logic. Read the primary source.
The textbook that shaped a generation. Terse and rigorous.

set_theory.label: Naive Set Theory
set_theory.desc: Sets, relations, functions, cardinality, Cantor diagonalisation. The raw vocabulary of every later subject.

books.n: ~100 pages. The single best week of self-study you can take.
Soviet rigour. Free PDF online. Concise.

books.t: Beiträge zur Begründung der transfiniten Mengenlehre
books.n: The original. Cantor builds the ordinals himself.

proof.label: Mathematical Proof
proof.desc: Direct, contradiction, contrapositive, induction, well-ordering. Writing mathematics, not computing it.

books.n: Best bridge from CS-mind to math-mind.

books.t: Problems in Plane and Solid Geometry
books.n: Soviet olympiad tradition — proof by doing. ~2000 problems.
The oldest proof tradition still in use. Books I–VI.

pred_logic.label: First-Order Logic
pred_logic.desc: Quantifiers, terms, models, validity, prenex normal form, completeness theorem. The formal language of all of mathematics and CS theory.

books.t: A Course in Mathematical Logic for Mathematicians
books.a: Yu. I. Manin (1977; 2e 2010 w/ Koblitz & Zilber)
books.n: Soviet. From a deep mathematician's view; 2e adds chapters on quantum computation and model theory.

number_systems.label: Number Systems (ℕ→ℝ)
number_systems.desc: Peano axioms, construction of ℤ, ℚ, ℝ via Dedekind cuts or Cauchy sequences. What "2+2=4" actually means.

books.t: Was sind und was sollen die Zahlen?
books.n: How ℕ is constructed. Primary source.
German rigour. ℕ to ℂ from scratch.

discrete_math.label: Discrete Mathematics
discrete_math.desc: Combinatorics, recurrences, generating functions, graph theory, asymptotics. The mathematics of finite structures.

books.n: The standard. Computer-scientist's discrete math, done with rigour.

books.a: J. H. van Lint & R. M. Wilson

books.t: Дискретная математика для программистов (Discrete Mathematics for Programmers)
books.n: Russian textbook based on St Petersburg lectures; algorithms on discrete structures.

calculus.label: Calculus (one variable)
calculus.desc: Limits, continuity, derivatives, Riemann integral, sequences and series — done rigorously, with ε-δ proofs.

books.n: Calculus as a first proof course. The standard for serious students.
Soviet tradition. More rigorous and complete than Spivak.

linear_algebra.label: Linear Algebra
linear_algebra.desc: Vector spaces, linear maps, eigenvalues, inner products, spectral theorem — coordinate-free.

books.a: A. I. Kostrikin & Yu. I. Manin
books.n: Soviet. Linear algebra fused with geometry.

elementary_geometry.label: Euclidean & Synthetic Geometry
elementary_geometry.desc: Plane and solid Euclidean geometry, ruler-and-compass constructions, similarity, the conics.

books.a: A. P. Kiselev (tr. Alexander Givental, Sumizdat 2006/2008)
books.n: The Russian school textbook. Used in the USSR for over a century.

books.a: H. S. M. Coxeter & S. L. Greitzer

elem_number_theory.label: Elementary Number Theory
elem_number_theory.desc: Divisibility, primes, congruences, quadratic reciprocity, Diophantine equations.

books.t: An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers
books.a: G. H. Hardy & E. M. Wright

books.n: Soviet master's textbook. Tight and brutal.

books.t: A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory

abstract_algebra.label: Abstract Algebra
abstract_algebra.desc: Groups, rings, fields, modules, Galois theory. The structural skeleton beneath all of mathematics.

books.n: Soviet master at MGU. Possibly the best single algebra book ever written.
Bourbaki-style reference. Necessary but not always pedagogical.

real_analysis.label: Real Analysis
real_analysis.desc: Metric spaces, sequences, continuity, differentiation, Riemann–Stieltjes integration, function spaces.

books.n: "Baby Rudin". The American gold standard. Terse.
Soviet/Russian. More complete than Rudin and arguably better.

multivar_calc.label: Multivariable & Vector Calculus
multivar_calc.desc: Differentiation in ℝⁿ, implicit/inverse function theorems, multiple integrals, line/surface integrals, Stokes.

books.n: 140 pages. Stokes' theorem on manifolds in chapter 5.

ode.label: Ordinary Differential Equations
ode.desc: Existence-uniqueness, linear systems, phase plane, stability, Hamiltonian flows.

books.n: Soviet. The most beautiful ODE book ever written. Geometric viewpoint throughout.

probability.label: Probability Theory
probability.desc: Measure-theoretic probability: (Ω,ℱ,P), random variables, LLN, CLT, conditional expectation, martingales.

books.n: Soviet. Stricter and deeper than Western analogues.

books.t: Foundations of the Theory of Probability
books.n: The 80-page paper that founded modern probability.

topology.label: General Topology
topology.desc: Open sets, continuity, compactness, connectedness, product/quotient spaces, Tychonoff. The home of "limit".

complex_analysis.label: Complex Analysis
complex_analysis.desc: Holomorphic functions, Cauchy theorem, residues, conformal maps, Riemann mapping. Where real analysis becomes magic.

books.t: Methods of the Theory of Functions of a Complex Variable
books.a: M. A. Lavrentiev & B. V. Shabat

measure_theory.label: Measure & Integration
measure_theory.desc: σ-algebras, Lebesgue measure, dominated convergence, Lᵖ spaces, Radon-Nikodym, Fubini-Tonelli.

books.t: Real Analysis: Modern Techniques and Their Applications

books.a: H. L. Royden & P. M. Fitzpatrick

functional_analysis.label: Functional Analysis
functional_analysis.desc: Banach/Hilbert spaces, bounded operators, Hahn-Banach, open mapping, spectral theorem.

books.t: Elements of the Theory of Functions and Functional Analysis
books.a: A. N. Kolmogorov & S. V. Fomin
books.n: Soviet. The most-used functional analysis book on Earth.

diff_geometry.label: Differential Geometry
diff_geometry.desc: Smooth manifolds, tangent bundles, differential forms, Riemannian metrics, curvature.

books.t: Differential Geometry of Curves and Surfaces

books.a: B. A. Dubrovin, A. T. Fomenko, S. P. Novikov
books.n: Soviet trilogy. Geometry from a physicist's viewpoint.

pde.label: Partial Differential Equations
pde.desc: Heat, wave, Laplace equations; Fourier methods; Sobolev spaces; weak solutions; method of characteristics.

books.a: A. N. Tikhonov & A. A. Samarskii

aristotle_organon.label: Aristotelian Logic (Organon)
aristotle_organon.desc: Syllogisms, categories, terms, contradiction, demonstration. The original logical canon, ~340 BC.

books.t: Organon (Categories, On Interpretation, Prior & Posterior Analytics, Topics, Sophistical Refutations)
books.n: Six works. Read in this order.

ancient_phil.label: Ancient Greek Philosophy
ancient_phil.desc: Pre-socratics, Plato's dialogues, Aristotle's metaphysics. The 800-year ground floor of the West.

books.n: Read after the shorter dialogues (Apology, Meno, Phaedo).

books.a: G. S. Kirk, J. E. Raven, M. Schofield

eastern_phil.label: Eastern Philosophy
eastern_phil.desc: Chinese (Confucius, Laozi, Zhuangzi), Indian (Upanishads, Buddhist Madhyamaka), classical Japanese. Intentionally unparalleled by Greek thought.

books.t: 道德經 (Dao De Jing / Tao Te Ching)
books.n: Read D. C. Lau or Robert Henricks translation.
D. C. Lau or Edward Slingerland translation.
Foundation of Mahāyāna Buddhist philosophy. Garfield translation.

books.t: A Source Book in Indian Philosophy

medieval_phil.label: Medieval Philosophy
medieval_phil.desc: Christian, Jewish and Islamic scholastic traditions. Augustine, Aquinas, Avicenna, Averroes, Maimonides.

books.n: Use Pegis edition or McDermott's Concise Translation.

books.a: Avicenna / Ibn Sīnā (c. 1027; Marmura tr. of the Metaphysics, BYU 2005)
books.n: Persian-Islamic philosophical encyclopaedia. Read the Metaphysics; the title means "healing" of ignorance, not medicine.

rationalism.label: Continental Rationalism
rationalism.desc: 17th-c. systems built from clear-and-distinct ideas: Descartes, Spinoza, Leibniz.

books.n: 80 pages. Read with Objections and Replies.
Hard. Worth a year. Use Curley translation.

empiricism.label: British Empiricism
empiricism.desc: 17th–18th c. counterpart: knowledge from sensation. Locke, Berkeley, Hume.

books.n: The system. Or his Enquiry as a digest.

books.t: Three Dialogues Between Hylas and Philonous

ethics.label: Ethics
ethics.desc: Virtue (Aristotle), deontology (Kant), utilitarianism (Mill, Bentham), modern metaethics.

books.t: Groundwork of the Metaphysics of Morals

political_phil.label: Political Philosophy
political_phil.desc: Hobbes, Locke, Rousseau, Marx, Rawls. The basis of legitimacy of the state.

epistemology.label: Epistemology
epistemology.desc: What knowledge is, how it is justified, how to answer the skeptic. JTB tradition → Gettier (1963) → reliabilism, virtue epistemology, knowledge-first (Williamson), Bayesian/formal epistemology, social epistemology. Foundationalism vs. coherentism; internalism vs. externalism; a priori/a posteriori. The hinge of every other rigorous discipline — what licenses belief.

books.n: The original "what is knowledge?" dialogue. Read with Cornford or Burnyeat commentary.
Foundationalism born here. The skeptic's gauntlet.
The problem of induction. Empiricist skepticism at its sharpest.
90 pages. Fastest rigorous entry into the modern picture.
Three pages that broke JTB. The inflection point of 20th-c. epistemology.
Animal vs. reflective knowledge; competence-based justification.
Knowledge-first programme. Knowledge as unanalyzable mental state.

books.t: Probability Theory: The Logic of Science
books.n: Bayesian/formal epistemology. Probability as extended logic.

kant.label: Kant
kant.desc: Critique of Pure Reason: synthesis of rationalism and empiricism. Phenomena/noumena, transcendental idealism, antinomies. THE pivot of modern philosophy.

books.n: Read Kemp Smith or Guyer & Wood translation. Plan a year.
Self-summary of the first Critique. Start here.

hegel.label: Hegel
hegel.desc: Phenomenology of Spirit, Logic. Dialectical method. The most ambitious 19th-century system.

books.n: Notoriously hard. Pinkard translation. Use a guide (e.g. Stern).

nietzsche.label: Nietzsche
nietzsche.desc: Genealogy of morals, eternal recurrence, will to power. The hammer of 19th-c. metaphysics.

phenomenology.label: Phenomenology (Husserl)
phenomenology.desc: Intentionality, the epoché, lifeworld. The descriptive science of consciousness.

analytic_phil.label: Analytic Philosophy
analytic_phil.desc: Frege–Russell–Wittgenstein–Carnap–Quine. Logic, language, meaning. The 20th-century Anglo tradition.

books.n: Numbered propositions. Read with Anscombe's introduction.

books.t: From a Logical Point of View

phil_lang.label: Philosophy of Language
phil_lang.desc: Sense vs. reference (Frege), rigid designators (Kripke), speech acts (Austin), implicature (Grice).

books.n: 20 pages. Sense and reference founded here.

books.t: Studies in the Way of Words

phil_science.label: Philosophy of Science
phil_science.desc: Falsifiability (Popper), paradigms (Kuhn), research programmes (Lakatos), realism vs. anti-realism.

books.n: Mathematics as discovery, in dialogue form.

phil_mind.label: Philosophy of Mind
phil_mind.desc: Mind-body, qualia, intentionality, functionalism, the hard problem, Chinese Room.

panini.label: Pāṇinian Grammar
panini.desc: Aṣṭādhyāyī (~5th c. BCE): the world's first generative grammar. ~4000 sūtras describing Sanskrit. Predates Western linguistics by 2300 years.

books.t: Pāṇini: His Work and Its Traditions

saussure.label: Structural Linguistics (Saussure)
saussure.desc: Signifier/signified, langue/parole, synchronic vs diachronic. The 1916 birth of modern linguistics.

books.n: Posthumously assembled lecture notes. Harris translation.

books.t: Course in General Linguistics: notes from the 3rd course
books.a: ed. Eisuke Komatsu, tr. Roy Harris

phonetics.label: Phonetics
phonetics.desc: Articulatory, acoustic, auditory phonetics. The IPA. The physical substrate of speech.

books.n: The standard introductory text. Audio supplement.

books.t: The Sounds of the World's Languages

phonology.label: Phonology
phonology.desc: Phonemes, distinctive features, autosegmental phonology, optimality theory. The cognitive sound system.

morphology.label: Morphology
morphology.desc: Word structure: morphemes, affixation, compounding, paradigms. Word-formation across languages.

syntax.label: Syntax
syntax.desc: Phrase structure, X-bar, movement, government and binding, minimalism. The grammatical architecture.

books.t: Aspects of the Theory of Syntax

semantics.label: Formal Semantics
semantics.desc: Truth conditions, λ-calculus for natural language, types, quantifier scope, intensionality.

books.t: The Proper Treatment of Quantification in Ordinary English

books.a: D. R. Dowty, R. E. Wall, S. Peters

pragmatics.label: Pragmatics
pragmatics.desc: Speech acts, presupposition, conversational implicature, deixis, politeness.

historical_ling.label: Historical Linguistics
historical_ling.desc: Sound change, the comparative method, Indo-European reconstruction, grammaticalisation.

books.t: Language History, Language Change, and Language Relationship
books.a: Hans Henrich Hock & Brian D. Joseph

typology.label: Linguistic Typology
typology.desc: Cross-linguistic variation in word order, morphology, alignment. Universals.

sicp.label: Programming as Discipline (SICP)
sicp.desc: Abstraction, recursion, higher-order functions, interpreters, register machines. Built bottom-up in Scheme.

books.t: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
books.a: H. Abelson & G. J. Sussman with J. Sussman
books.n: MIT 6.001. The single best CS-philosophy book.

books.t: Concepts, Techniques, and Models of Computer Programming

data_struct.label: Data Structures
data_struct.desc: Arrays, lists, trees, hash tables, heaps, balanced search trees, persistent structures, amortised analysis.

books.t: The Design and Analysis of Computer Algorithms
books.a: A. Aho, J. Hopcroft, J. Ullman (1974)
books.n: Original integration of structures and analysis.

books.t: The Art of Computer Programming, Vol. 1

algorithms.label: Algorithms
algorithms.desc: Correctness, asymptotic analysis, divide-and-conquer, dynamic programming, graph algorithms, NP-hardness.

books.a: T. H. Cormen, C. E. Leiserson, R. L. Rivest, C. Stein

books.t: The Art of Computer Programming, Vols. 1–4A
books.n: TAOCP. Read what your interest demands.

formal_lang.label: Formal Languages & Automata
formal_lang.desc: Regular and context-free languages, pushdown automata, Chomsky hierarchy, parsing theory.

books.t: Introduction to Automata Theory, Languages, and Computation
Introduction to the Theory of Computation
books.n: More elegant. Read both parts I and II here as well as in computability.

computability.label: Computability
computability.desc: Turing machines, Church–Turing thesis, halting problem, recursion theorem, μ-recursive functions.

books.t: Introduction to the Theory of Computation

books.a: G. Boolos, J. Burgess, R. Jeffrey

complexity.label: Computational Complexity
complexity.desc: P, NP, polynomial hierarchy, space complexity, randomised classes, interactive proofs, PCP.

type_theory.label: Type Theory & PL
type_theory.desc: Simply-typed λ-calculus, polymorphism, dependent types, Curry-Howard. The mathematics of programming languages.

books.a: Per Martin-Löf (Bibliopolis 1984; notes by Giovanni Sambin from 1980 Padua lectures)
books.n: Source of dependent type theory. ~91 pages.

compilers.label: Compilers
compilers.desc: Lexing, parsing, IR, type checking, optimisation, code generation. From source to silicon.

books.t: Compilers: Principles, Techniques and Tools (Dragon Book)
books.a: A. V. Aho, M. S. Lam, R. Sethi, J. D. Ullman

comp_arch.label: Computer Architecture
comp_arch.desc: ISA, pipelining, caches, memory hierarchy, parallelism, GPUs.

books.a: J. L. Hennessy & D. A. Patterson

os.label: Operating Systems
os.desc: Processes, threads, scheduling, virtual memory, file systems, synchronisation, virtualisation.

books.t: The Design and Implementation of the FreeBSD Operating System

networks.label: Computer Networks
networks.desc: OSI/TCP-IP layers, routing, congestion control, transport, application layer.

books.a: Andrew S. Tanenbaum & D. J. Wetherall
J. F. Kurose & K. W. Ross

databases.label: Databases
databases.desc: Relational model, SQL, transactions/ACID, indexing, query optimisation, distributed DBs.

books.a: H. Garcia-Molina, J. D. Ullman, J. Widom

distributed_sys.label: Distributed Systems
distributed_sys.desc: Consensus, replication, CAP, time and order (Lamport), fault tolerance, Paxos/Raft.

books.t: Time, Clocks, and the Ordering of Events

cryptography.label: Cryptography
cryptography.desc: Provable security, symmetric/asymmetric, hash functions, zero-knowledge, lattice crypto.

ai_aima.label: AI: A Modern Approach
ai_aima.desc: The field-defining synthesis: agents, search, KR, planning, probabilistic reasoning, learning, NLP, robotics.

books.n: AIMA. The single canonical AI textbook. Cover to cover.

search_planning.label: Search & Heuristics
search_planning.desc: Uninformed search, A*, IDA*, heuristic admissibility/consistency, AND-OR graphs.

books.t: Heuristics: Intelligent Search Strategies for Computer Problem Solving
Artificial Intelligence: Structures and Strategies for Complex Problem Solving

knowledge_repr.label: Knowledge Representation
knowledge_repr.desc: Description logics, frames, semantic networks, ontologies, situation/event calculi, non-monotonic reasoning.

books.a: R. J. Brachman & H. J. Levesque

logic_prog.label: Logic Programming
logic_prog.desc: Horn clauses, SLD-resolution, Prolog, answer-set programming. Declarative computation.

prob_reasoning.label: Probabilistic Reasoning
prob_reasoning.desc: Bayes nets, exact inference, variable elimination, sum-product, sampling. Reasoning under uncertainty.

books.n: The book that put Bayes nets on the map.
Causal calculus, do-operator. The paradigm shift.

bayesian_nets.label: Probabilistic Graphical Models
bayesian_nets.desc: Bayes nets, Markov random fields, factor graphs, learning structure and parameters.

books.t: Probabilistic Graphical Models: Principles and Techniques

reinforcement.label: Reinforcement Learning
reinforcement.desc: MDPs, dynamic programming, TD, Q-learning, policy gradients, actor-critic.

books.a: Richard S. Sutton & Andrew G. Barto

planning_ai.label: Automated Planning
planning_ai.desc: STRIPS, PDDL, partial-order planning, hierarchical task networks, planning under uncertainty.

books.a: M. Ghallab, D. Nau, P. Traverso

multi_agent.label: Multi-Agent Systems
multi_agent.desc: Game theory, mechanism design, distributed constraint optimisation, social choice.

cognitive_sci.label: Cognitive Science
cognitive_sci.desc: Vision, memory, attention; computational levels (Marr); embodiment; ACT-R.

agi.label: AGI / Universal AI
agi.desc: AIXI, Solomonoff induction, Kolmogorov complexity, theoretical foundations of agency.

books.t: An Introduction to Kolmogorov Complexity and Its Applications

info_theory.label: Information Theory
info_theory.desc: Entropy, mutual information, channel capacity, source/channel coding, rate-distortion. Shannon's quantitative theory of communication and inference.

books.n: The 55-page paper that founded the field. Read the original.

books.a: Thomas M. Cover & Joy A. Thomas

books.t: Information Theory, Inference, and Learning Algorithms
books.n: Bayesian flavour, free PDF online. Bridges to ML.

nlp.label: Natural Language Processing
nlp.desc: Tokenisation, parsing, language models, machine translation, semantic parsing, dialogue. Both classical/statistical and neural.

books.n: The textbook. 3e draft chapters free online; covers classical to neural.

books.t: Foundations of Statistical Natural Language Processing

books.n: Modern, free draft. Strong on linguistic structure.

computer_vision.label: Computer Vision
computer_vision.desc: Image formation, filtering, features, multi-view geometry, segmentation, recognition. Classical pipelines and learned representations.

books.n: The standard textbook. Free PDF online.

books.t: Multiple View Geometry in Computer Vision

robotics.label: Robotics
robotics.desc: Kinematics and dynamics, motion planning, SLAM, state estimation, manipulation. Where perception, planning and control meet a body.

books.a: Sebastian Thrun, Wolfram Burgard & Dieter Fox

books.t: Modern Robotics: Mechanics, Planning, and Control
books.a: Kevin M. Lynch & Frank C. Park
books.n: Free PDF; geometric kinematics from screws and Lie groups.

books.a: B. Siciliano, L. Sciavicco, L. Villani & G. Oriolo

automated_reasoning.label: Automated Reasoning
automated_reasoning.desc: Resolution, unification, SAT, SMT, theorem proving, model checking. Mechanising logic.

books.t: A Machine-Oriented Logic Based on the Resolution Principle
books.n: The paper that made automated theorem proving practical.

books.t: Handbook of Practical Logic and Automated Reasoning
books.n: Single-volume integration of logic, decision procedures, theorem proving — with OCaml code.

books.a: ed. A. Biere, M. Heule, H. van Maaren, T. Walsh

game_playing.label: Game Playing & Adversarial Search
game_playing.desc: Minimax, alpha-beta pruning, transposition tables, MCTS, evaluation functions. The classical AI of chess, Go, poker.

books.a: Donald E. Knuth & Ronald W. Moore (1975)

books.t: A Survey of Monte Carlo Tree Search Methods
One Jump Ahead: Computer Perfection at Checkers
books.n: First-hand account of solving checkers; the Chinook story.

connectionism.label: Connectionism
connectionism.desc: Perceptrons, Hopfield nets, backprop, distributed representations. The neural-network tradition predating deep learning.

books.t: Principles of Neurodynamics: Perceptrons and the Theory of Brain Mechanisms

books.n: The famous limit-of-perceptrons proof. Triggered the first AI winter.

books.a: David E. Rumelhart, James L. McClelland & the PDP Research Group (1986)
books.n: The connectionist revival; backprop popularised here.

statistical_learning.label: Statistical Learning
statistical_learning.desc: Supervised/unsupervised learning, bias-variance, regularisation, kernels, VC theory, PAC learning.

books.a: Trevor Hastie, Robert Tibshirani & Jerome Friedman
books.n: ESL. The standard reference; free PDF.

books.t: The Nature of Statistical Learning Theory
books.n: VC dimension, structural risk minimisation, SVMs from the source.

books.a: Mehryar Mohri, Afshin Rostamizadeh & Ameet Talwalkar

books.n: Pre-deep-learning standard; concept learning, decision trees.

deep_learning.label: Deep Learning
deep_learning.desc: Backprop at scale, convolutional nets, recurrence, attention, transformers, generative models.

books.a: Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio & Aaron Courville (2016)
Yann LeCun, Yoshua Bengio & Geoffrey Hinton (Nature, 2015)

books.n: Modern, free PDF; covers transformers and diffusion models.

books.a: Aston Zhang, Zachary C. Lipton, Mu Li & Alexander J. Smola

decision_theory.label: Decision Theory
decision_theory.desc: Expected utility, von Neumann–Morgenstern axioms, Savage's axiomatisation, Bayesian decision-making, evidential vs. causal decision theory.

books.n: Subjective probability and decision theory founded together.

books.t: Theory of Games and Economic Behavior
The Foundations of Causal Decision Theory
books.n: The CDT defence. Modern philosophical decision theory.

ai_safety.label: AI Safety & Alignment
ai_safety.desc: Specification, robustness, interpretability, reward hacking, scalable oversight. The technical agenda of building AI systems whose objectives match ours.

books.t: Human Compatible: Artificial Intelligence and the Problem of Control
books.n: AIMA's co-author on assistance games and inverse reward design.
The orthogonality thesis and instrumental convergence at length.

books.a: Dario Amodei, Chris Olah, Jacob Steinhardt, Paul Christiano, John Schulman & Dan Mané (2016)

books.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
books.a: Paul F. Christiano, Jan Leike, Tom B. Brown, Miljan Martic, Shane Legg & Dario Amodei (2017)
books.n: RLHF; the alignment-via-feedback method that scaled to LLMs.

feynman_lectures.label: General Physics (Feynman)
feynman_lectures.desc: Mechanics, E&M, quantum, statistical — at the level of feel-the-truth physics. The pedagogical anchor.

books.t: The Feynman Lectures on Physics (3 vols.)
books.a: R. P. Feynman, R. B. Leighton, M. Sands
books.n: Free online at Caltech. Read all 3 volumes.
Soviet. The hardest problem book; canonical for top Russian undergrads.

classical_mech.label: Classical Mechanics
classical_mech.desc: Lagrangian and Hamiltonian mechanics, symplectic geometry, rigid bodies, perturbation.

books.a: L. D. Landau & E. M. Lifshitz
books.n: ~200 pages. The most concentrated mechanics book ever written.
Soviet. Differential-geometric mechanics from a master.

electromagnetism.label: Electromagnetism
electromagnetism.desc: Maxwell's equations, gauges, radiation, Lorentz invariance.

books.t: The Classical Theory of Fields (Course of Theoretical Physics, Vol. 2)
books.a: L. D. Landau & E. M. Lifshitz
books.n: Special and general relativity included. Soviet rigour.

thermodynamics.label: Thermodynamics
thermodynamics.desc: Laws of thermodynamics, entropy, free energies, phase transitions.

books.t: Thermodynamics and an Introduction to Thermostatistics

quantum_mech.label: Quantum Mechanics
quantum_mech.desc: Hilbert spaces, observables, Schrödinger equation, perturbation theory, scattering, identical particles.

books.t: Quantum Mechanics: Non-Relativistic Theory (Course of Theoretical Physics, Vol. 3)
books.a: L. D. Landau & E. M. Lifshitz
books.n: Soviet. Tighter than any Western text.

statistical_mech.label: Statistical Mechanics
statistical_mech.desc: Ensembles, partition functions, Bose/Fermi/Maxwell-Boltzmann statistics, phase transitions.

books.t: Statistical Physics, Part 1 (Course of Theoretical Physics, Vol. 5)
books.a: L. D. Landau & E. M. Lifshitz

books.t: Fundamentals of Statistical and Thermal Physics

relativity.label: General Relativity
relativity.desc: Curved spacetime, Einstein field equations, Schwarzschild, FLRW cosmology.

books.t: The Classical Theory of Fields (latter half)
books.a: L. D. Landau & E. M. Lifshitz
C. W. Misner, K. S. Thorne, J. A. Wheeler (MTW)

solid_state.label: Solid State Physics
solid_state.desc: Crystals, band theory, phonons, semiconductors, superconductivity.

books.a: Neil W. Ashcroft & N. David Mermin

qft.label: Quantum Field Theory
qft.desc: Path integrals, Feynman diagrams, gauge theory, renormalisation. The deep theory.

books.t: An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory
books.a: Michael E. Peskin & Daniel V. Schroeder

books.t: Quantum Electrodynamics (Course of Theoretical Physics, Vol. 4)
books.a: V. B. Berestetskii, E. M. Lifshitz, L. P. Pitaevskii

optics.label: Optics
optics.desc: Wave optics, diffraction, polarisation, coherence, Fourier optics.

books.n: The bible of classical optics. German-Polish-British rigour.

digital_logic.label: Digital Logic
digital_logic.desc: Boolean algebra, combinational and sequential circuits, FSMs, FPGAs.

books.a: M. Morris Mano & M. D. Ciletti
D. M. Harris & S. L. Harris

circuit_theory.label: Circuit Theory
circuit_theory.desc: Kirchhoff laws, RLC, phasors, transient analysis, two-port networks, S-parameters.

books.a: W. H. Hayt, J. E. Kemmerly, S. M. Durbin
Charles A. Desoer & Ernest S. Kuh

art_electronics.label: Practical Electronics
art_electronics.desc: Op-amps, transistors, ADCs/DACs, analog and digital design from the bench.

books.n: The single most important practical electronics book ever written.

books.t: Learning the Art of Electronics: A Hands-On Lab Course

microelectronics.label: Microelectronics
microelectronics.desc: Transistor-level design (CMOS), differential amplifiers, mixed-signal, op-amp internals.

books.a: Adel S. Sedra & Kenneth C. Smith

books.t: Design of Analog CMOS Integrated Circuits
